main_thesis: aggregate gain alone is insufficient to explain revision effects; observer quality does not automatically become controller gain under the tested setup

candidates.title: Benefit-Bucket / Recoverability Analysis
candidates.summary: Use fixed/harmed/no-effect decomposition and representative cases to explain who revision helps, hurts, or leaves unchanged.

candidates.title: Runtime-Lineage / Honest-Compute Protocol
candidates.summary: Provide auditable runtime fairness, claim-to-asset mapping, and observer/controller reporting discipline to prevent implementation confounds.

candidates.title: Observer-Controller Split
candidates.summary: Keep as a scoped falsification-style framing claim, not as an independent execution lane or hero contribution.

candidates.title: Minimal Honest-Compute + Bucket Audit Paper
candidates.summary: Fallback if the higher-level observer/controller framing remains too evidence-thin.

candidates.title: Reasoning-vs-Code Boundary Audit
candidates.summary: Fallback if boundary evidence becomes stronger than the broader revision-effect story.

explicitly_dropped: Minimal Controller as a serious candidate
